item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently reject automatic priority of society over individual rights from opening to final turn. Both acknowledge that public or shared interests matter, but only within rights-based limits, so there is no visible stance shift or flip. Engagement is moderate: they echo and develop the same abuse-of-power, minority-protection, and rights-as-boundary frame rather than directly rebutting a live disagreement.
